_doc: OPERATOR presentation overlay (commerce + presentation, operator-owned). The soverella emitter MERGES the machine-made engine (content + routing) with this overlay to produce engine.json in the runtime's terminals schema. Bee-D never sets tier/severity. Dishes take heading from the menu label and result_copy from the grounded result_state unless overridden here. Escapes REQUIRE heading + result_copy here (the engine carries none). tier=67 is FORCED on every escape by the translator regardless of this file. SEED map (old terminal -> new state) is a PROPOSAL for operator approval at the diff HOLD.

dasp-id-requirements-high-balance._seed_from: R2/R1 balance:ge5k modifier (no direct old terminal)

none_fit.heading: DASP may not apply to your situation
none_fit.result_copy: Your answers suggest DASP may not fit — for example you are still in Australia, or you held permanent residence or citizenship, so the ordinary Australian super rules apply instead. A short personalised review works through what does apply to your circumstances and the steps to take next.

insufficient_information.heading: Your situation needs a closer look
insufficient_information.result_copy: Your answers do not yet confirm which DASP position applies to you — a key detail (such as your visa type, balance, or status) is unconfirmed. A short personalised review confirms where you stand and exactly what to check before you claim.

high_complexity.heading: A more complex situation
high_complexity.result_copy: Your answers point to more than one factor at play (for example mixed visa types, or more than one timeline concern), so a single clean answer does not apply. A short personalised review works through the interacting parts of your situation before you claim.
